description:
Long time ago, when I was just a little goproblem child, I tried to make this kind of problem with double playing white. I failed and someone commented it as a very stupid idea. Today, with more experience, IÂ´m comming back to face this kind of challenge again. I will make a double-playing-white problem that works!
**
Please note that in Japanese-rules-seki white can easily capture now because she has two moves in a row.
sgf:
(;AB[je]AB[ke]AB[le]AB[lf]AB[lg]AB[lh]AB[li]AB[ki]AB[ji]AB[ii]AB[hi]AB[gi]AB[gh]AB[gg]AB[ge]AB[gf]AB[he]AB[ie]AW[kd]AW[ld]AW[md]AW[me]AW[mf]AW[mg]AW[mh]AW[mi]AW[lj]AW[kj]AW[jj]AW[ij]AW[gj]AW[hj]AW[fj]AW[fi]AW[fh]AW[fg]AW[ff]AW[fe]AW[fd]AW[gd]AW[hd]AW[id]AW[jd]AW[mj]AW[kf]C[Black to play and live. After each blacks move white has two moves in a row!]AP[goproblems]
(;B[kg]AW[jg];W[jh]
(;B[jf]AW[ih];W[hg]
(;B[if]C[Good, white cannot capture black any more! RIGHT])
(;B[hf]AW[if];W[hh]C[Black cannot win this ko - white can anytime just say pass and take. And when black runs out of threats, white can play L12 and L14 to capture.])
(;B[hh]AW[if]C[Specific form of seki - black does not live with points, but ok, you solved it :)RIGHT]))
(;B[hg]AW[if];W[ih]
(;B[jf]C[Good! White cannot win anymore.RIGHT])
(;B[ig]AW[jf];W[kh]C[Not a seki, black is dead, next turn white will play both places to capture.]))
